metadata.name: WordPress Core Execution Tests - Mail and URL Handling
metadata.description: Code generation tasks for WordPress Mail and URL Handling APIs

tests.prompt: Implement `wpbp_mail_url_001( string $url ): string` to sanitize a URL for storage or redirects while preferring HTTPS when WordPress adds a missing scheme.
tests.expected_behavior: `wpbp_mail_url_001()` uses WordPress URL sanitization in a non-display context with HTTPS first in the allowed protocol list. A schemeless host should gain `https://`, an existing `http://` URL should remain HTTP, and unsafe protocols should be rejected.
requirements: Allow both HTTPS and HTTP with HTTPS preferred

required_patterns.description: Supplies HTTPS as the preferred protocol

assertions.code: if ( ! function_exists( 'wpbp_mail_url_001' ) ) { return false; } return 'https://example.org/path?x=1&y=2' === wpbp_mail_url_001( 'example.org/path?x=1&y=2' ) && 'http://example.org/path' === wpbp_mail_url_001( 'http://example.org/path' ) && '' === wpbp_mail_url_001( 'javascript:alert(1)' );
assertions.description: Schemeless URLs prefer HTTPS, existing HTTP is preserved, and disallowed protocols are removed

tests.reference_solution: function wpbp_mail_url_001( string $url ): string { return sanitize_url( $url, array( 'https', 'http' ) ); }

tests.prompt: Implement `wpbp_mail_url_002( string $email ): string|false` to validate an email address and return WordPress's validation result.
tests.expected_behavior: `wpbp_mail_url_002()` delegates to `is_email()`, returning the original email string on success and `false` on failure. The test focuses on WordPress's return contract, not boolean-only validation or custom normalization.
requirements: Return the successful email string, not boolean true

assertions.code: if ( ! function_exists( 'wpbp_mail_url_002' ) ) { return false; } return 'Person+tag@example.co.uk' === wpbp_mail_url_002( 'Person+tag@example.co.uk' ) && false === wpbp_mail_url_002( 'bad@' ) && false === wpbp_mail_url_002( 'person@example' );
assertions.description: Valid addresses are returned unchanged and invalid addresses return false

tests.reference_solution: function wpbp_mail_url_002( string $email ): string|false { return is_email( $email ); }

tests.prompt: Implement `wpbp_mail_url_003( string $location ): string` to sanitize a redirect target before it is passed to a redirect function.
tests.expected_behavior: `wpbp_mail_url_003()` uses `wp_sanitize_redirect()` and returns the sanitized location string without sending headers. It should encode spaces and strip CR/LF sequences; allowed-host validation belongs to `wp_safe_redirect()` or `wp_validate_redirect()`, not this helper.
requirements: Return the sanitized location without performing a redirect

assertions.code: if ( ! function_exists( 'wpbp_mail_url_003' ) ) { return false; } $spaced = wpbp_mail_url_003( 'https://example.com/a b' ); $header = wpbp_mail_url_003( 'https://example.com/%0d%0Aadmin' ); return 'https://example.com/a%20b' === $spaced && 'https://example.com/admin' === $header;
assertions.description: Redirect sanitization encodes spaces and removes CR/LF sequences

tests.reference_solution: function wpbp_mail_url_003( string $url ): string { return wp_sanitize_redirect( $url ); }

tests.prompt: Implement `wpbp_mail_url_004( string $to, string $subject, string $html, array $embeds ): bool` to send an HTML email through WordPress with embedded image paths.
tests.expected_behavior: `wpbp_mail_url_004()` calls `wp_mail()` with the supplied recipient, subject, HTML body, a text/html Content-Type header, no attachments, and the supplied embeds array. The verifier short-circuits delivery with `pre_wp_mail` and checks the arguments passed to WordPress.
requirements: Use wp_mail() rather than PHPMailer directly
Pass the supplied embeds array to wp_mail() and return its boolean result

required_patterns.description: Passes embedded image paths through wp_mail

assertions.code:
if ( ! function_exists( 'wpbp_mail_url_004' ) ) { return false; } $embeds = array( 'logo' => '/tmp/wpbp-logo.png' ); $html = '

'; $sent = wpbp_mail_url_004( 'nobody@example.com', 'Embedded', $html, $embeds ); $seen = $GLOBALS['wpbp_mail_url_004_seen'] ?? array(); if ( true !== $sent || 1 !== count( $seen ) ) { return false; } $atts = $seen[0]; $headers = $atts['headers'] ?? array(); if ( is_string( $headers ) ) { $headers = array( $headers ); } $has_html_header = false; foreach ( $headers as $header ) { if ( is_string( $header ) && false !== stripos( $header, 'Content-Type' ) && false !== stripos( $header, 'text/html' ) ) { $has_html_header = true; } } return 'nobody@example.com' === ( $atts['to'] ?? null ) && 'Embedded' === ( $atts['subject'] ?? null ) && $html === ( $atts['message'] ?? null ) && $embeds === ( $atts['embeds'] ?? null ) && $has_html_header;
assertions.description: wp_mail receives the supplied message fields, HTML header, and embeds array

tests.reference_solution: function wpbp_mail_url_004( string $to, string $subject, string $html, array $embeds ): bool { return wp_mail( $to, $subject, $html, array( 'Content-Type: text/html; charset=UTF-8' ), array(), $embeds ); }
